message:
fix: enforce session ownership on cancel and dispatch WS handlers async (#9724)

Fixes #9712, Fixes #9713

## Issue #9712 — Unauthorized Mission Cancellation (CSRF/Bypass)

handleCancelChat accepted any sessionId without verifying the
requesting WebSocket connection owned that session. A malicious client
could cancel any active mission by sending a cancel_chat message with
a known sessionId belonging to a different user.

Fix: introduce activeChatEntry, which binds each registered session to
the *websocket.Conn that started it. handleCancelChat now compares the
requesting conn against the stored conn pointer; mismatches are rejected
with an "unauthorized_cancel" error response and a security-level log.
The HTTP cancel path (handleCancelChatHTTP) is already guarded by
validateToken and is unaffected.

## Issue #9713 — WebSocket Reader Blocking on Hung Providers

The catch-all else branch in handleWebSocket dispatched handleMessage
synchronously inside the read loop. If any handler in that branch took
time (e.g. agent lookup, cluster queries) the read loop was blocked,
preventing pings and cancel_chat messages from being processed.

Fix: move the else branch into a semaphore-bounded goroutine, matching
the existing pattern for TypeChat/TypeClaude/TypeKubectl. The semaphore
(maxWSGoroutines) prevents unbounded goroutine creation. The closed
flag is checked before writing the response to avoid races on disconnect.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -176,8 +176,11 @@ type Server struct {
 	backendCmd *exec.Cmd
 	backendMux sync.Mutex
 
-	// Active chat cancel functions — maps sessionID → cancel for in-progress chats
-	activeChatCtxs   map[string]context.CancelFunc
+	// activeChatEntry binds an in-progress session's cancel function to the
+	// WebSocket connection that started it (#9712). handleCancelChat uses the
+	// conn pointer to verify the requester owns the session before cancelling,
+	// preventing cross-session CSRF/bypass attacks.
+	activeChatCtxs   map[string]activeChatEntry
 	activeChatCtxsMu sync.Mutex
 
 	// dryRunSessions tracks session IDs that are in dry-run mode.
@@ -294,7 +297,7 @@ func NewServer(cfg Config) (*Server, error) {
 		agentToken:        agentToken,
 		sessionStart:      now,
 		todayDate:         now.Format("2006-01-02"),
-		activeChatCtxs:    make(map[string]context.CancelFunc),
+		activeChatCtxs:    make(map[string]activeChatEntry),
 		dryRunSessions:    make(map[string]bool),
 		sessionTokenQuota: sessionQuota,
 	}
